content: Ahtar: Damn fine evening, ain't it? Perfect weather for beheadings—blood don't freeze like last winter when my axe stuck mid-swing. Had to kick the bastard's head loose like a frozen horker turd. Jala: Ugh, keep that talk out of my house, you brute. I just scrubbed the floors. Besides, you know I don’t like thinking about your work when I’m trying to sell fresh apples to nobles. Makes ‘em think I’ve got bloodstains on my melons. Ahtar: Ha! Like they care. apprentices

Ahtar: Aye, fair point. Nobles wouldn't know bloodstains from wine stains anyway—too busy sipping Elswyr vintage and groping servants. Jala: Hmph. Just promise me you’ll wash up before rolling into my bed tonight.
